steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes that (1) Northbridge United wears red and wins more home matches than Riverside, whose uniform is not red; (2) research shows red triggers associations with power/dominance and causes caution/hesitation in observers. The final sentence says: "These findings, if applicable in the context of athletic competitions, could suggest that in matches played at Northbridge United's home stadium, opponents may have ______". The blank must name the psychological effect research predicts red would have on opponents — a subconscious influence caused by the uniform color.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — been unaffected by the red clothing worn by fans in the stadium.: contradicts the entire point of citing the research — if findings apply, opponents would be affected, not unaffected. ✗
  • Choice B — played more aggressively than they would have if their own uniforms were red.: introduces opponents' own uniforms, which the passage never discusses. ✗
  • Choice C — been influenced by the color of Northbridge United's uniforms.: directly applies the research finding (red causes psychological responses) to opponents facing Northbridge United's red uniforms. ✓
  • Choice D — been aware that Northbridge United's uniform color affected their own performance.: the passage describes a subconscious response (like reacting to a stop sign), not conscious awareness. ✗
That is the correct answer.
